data.body:
OVERLAY CATALOG — internationalization / localization (i18n/l10n)  [PROJECT-DEPENDENT]
- Source: eslint-plugin-formatjs rule taxonomy (MIT; ~18 ICU/message defects = rubric backbone) + eslint-plugin-i18next (MIT; hardcoded-string detection) + Unicode CLDR / ICU MessageFormat (Unicode-3.0, permissive; plural categories + locale formatting) + W3C i18n best practices (W3C license). No turnkey LLM rubric; author from these.
- Dimensions: A) externalization (no hardcoded user-facing strings; route via t()/FormattedMessage; NO sentence concatenation); B) ICU correctness (plural/selectordinal w/ CLDR categories; other-branch present; valid ICU; placeholders supplied; avoid nested/multi-plural); C) message metadata/translator context (stable id; description/translator comment; no emoji); D) locale-aware formatting (Intl.DateTimeFormat/NumberFormat; currency; locale-aware collation/case — no naive toLowerCase); E) bidi/RTL (logical start/end not left/right; text-expansion tolerance; bidi-safe interpolation); F) encoding (UTF-8; normalization; no 1char=1byte); G) language tagging (BCP-47; lang/dir).
- Triggers: locale files (*.po/*.pot/*.arb/*.xliff/*.xlf; messages*.properties; {locales,i18n,lang,translations}/**.{json,yaml,yml}; *.strings; values*/strings.xml; *.ftl path-gated) + translation call-site signals (t(), FormattedMessage, gettext, ngettext, $t, trans()) when an i18n lib is imported.
- Activation rule: (locale-glob match) OR (i18n-lib import present) OR (explicit base-escalation). Do NOT fire on a lone string literal.
- Base-escalation: user-facing string changes / hardcoded literals / sentence concatenation in a project WITHOUT established i18n.
- RELEVANCE FLAG: project-dependent — HIGH for user-facing apps, LOW/NEAR-ZERO for backend/CLI/library with no user-facing strings (incl. rebar itself). Suppress on non-UI repos.
- LICENSE: formatjs/i18next/i18n-ally MIT, CLDR/ICU Unicode-3.0, W3C permissive — all usable. FLAG: GNU gettext MANUAL is GFDL (copyleft) -> restate conventions, don't copy text; vendor blog rubrics proprietary -> orientation only.
